text: When starting a project, it is essential to set clear goals to guide your team and measure success. One effective method is to use SMART goals, which means making your objectives specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you might set a goal like "increase page load speed by 20% within three months." To stay on track, break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing a prototype by the first month. Each milestone should produce clear deliverables, like a report or a design. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved, so all expectations match and the team works together smoothly.
